description: Counterpart of the exam-style case agents-exam/adv-many-tools-still-wf.json ({wf, false}): seven integrations, zero branches — tool count alone must not produce an agent. Also probes the node-choice bias: an AI Agent node orchestrating the seven fixed steps would be the tool-count variant of the vocabulary prior. Plan-first (2026-07-09): the assistant proposes the approach; the build is never exercised and expectations avoid build-tool coupling.

conversation.text: For each new signup: verify email, check against blocklist, add to Postgres, send welcome email, add to Mailchimp, notify sales in Slack, log to Segment. Don't build anything yet — first walk me through how you'd set this up.

text: [Do not approve any plan, setup card, or build confirmation.
If the assistant asks a clarifying question, give a minimal plausible answer.
Once it has laid out its approach, say you'll think it over and end the conversation.]

processExpectations: The agent proposes a workflow of fixed steps across the seven integrations, and does not propose an n8n Agent or an orchestrating agent step despite the number of services involved.
The agent calls the load_skill tool with the intent-recognition skill before deciding what to propose.
